title: Mass Concrete Pour Hydration Thermal Gradient Controller
description: Monitors temperatures at eight depths through a mass concrete pour using DS18B20 probes, computes the core-to-surface differential ΔT, and drives a recirculating water cooling jacket via SSR and PID control to keep ΔT < 20 °C per ACI 305R — preventing thermal cracking during curing of 1–2 m thick sections.
objective: Closed-loop thermal management of mass concrete hydration to satisfy ACI 305R ΔT ≤ 20 °C, with alarm at 18 °C and fault at 22 °C, hourly SD log.
constraints: Arduino Mega, eight DS18B20 probes on 1-Wire bus at 50–850 mm depths, 25 A SSR, 12 V circulation pump, SD module; section thickness 1.5 m.
supporting_fact_titles: Concrete Hydration Exotherm and Thermal Gradient Limits
design_element_titles: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller

element_usage_notes.DS18B20 1-Wire Temperature Probe Array: Eight probes at graduated depths; index 0 = core, indices 1-7 = surface zone; ΔT = T_core − mean(T_surface).
element_usage_notes.SSR-Controlled Water Cooling Jacket: SSR modulates circulation pump duty cycle to extract heat from concrete core.
element_usage_notes.Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controlle: Outer loop: ΔT setpoint 15 °C; inner loop: cooling water outlet temperature rate.
element_usage_notes.Buzzer and LED Fault Annunciator: Warning at ΔT = 18 °C, fault alarm at ΔT = 22 °C; both independent of PID loop.

element_interactions.to: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: Core-surface differential drives outer PID loop process variable.

element_interactions.from: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: PID output sets SSR duty cycle to modulate pump flow and cooling rate.
Direct threshold comparison triggers alarm independently of PID loop.
